title: Malaxis seramica
taxonomy: Malaxis seramica is a species of orchid. It belongs to the subtribe Malaxidinae, within the orchid family Orchidaceae. The genus Malaxis is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae, which is a subfamily of the orchid family. Malaxis seramica is a member of the family Orchidaceae, which contains approximately 30,000 known species of orchids. The classification of Malaxis seramica is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the clade Monocots.

intro: Malaxis seramica, also known as the Seram Malaxis orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid belonging to the family Orchidaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis seramica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Malaxis seramica is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or decoction to treat a range of health ailments.
